message:
feat: flip ssh, termwiz-funcs, mux-server defaults from async-smol to async-asupersync

Continues the workspace-wide async runtime migration (wa-brc7d epic):

- frankenterm-ssh: default feature async-smol → async-asupersync
  (full dual-backend support already implemented with runtime abstraction
  layer and compile-time guards; 40 tests pass with both backends)
- termwiz-funcs: default feature async-smol → async-asupersync
- frankenterm-mux-server: async_ossl dep feature async-io → async-asupersync

Verified: all three crates compile cleanly via rch. The mux crate (which
uses frankenterm-ssh with default-features=false) is unaffected.

wa-brc7d.4 (config), wa-brc7d.5 (mux), wa-brc7d.6 (ssh) are all
effectively complete — each uses the promise abstraction layer with
zero direct smol/async-io imports.

Remaining: wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl have direct
smol::Async<T> usage requiring API design work (tracked as ft-riz42).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ publish = false
 # See more keys and their definitions at https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html
 
 [features]
-default = ["lua", "async-smol"]
+default = ["lua", "async-asupersync"]
 lua = ["dep:luahelper", "dep:mlua", "config/lua"]
 async-smol = ["config/async-smol"]
 async-asupersync = ["config/async-asupersync"]

@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@ publish = false
 # See more keys and their definitions at https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html
 
 [features]
-default = ["libssh-rs", "ssh2", "async-smol"]
+default = ["libssh-rs", "ssh2", "async-asupersync"]
 async-smol = ["dep:smol", "async_ossl/async-io", "frankenterm-uds/async-io"]
 async-asupersync = [
     "dep:asupersync",
